"Why does my AI output sound so generic?"

Because you're prompting at the surface.

You ask for a strategy, AI gives you one, you move on. You never ask "and then what happens?"

That one missing question separates average AI work from exceptional. And it has a name. https://t.co/h1IY06quEO

Howard Marks built Oaktree Capital into one of the largest investment firms in the world.

His core thinking tool: Second-Order Thinking.

First-order thinkers see the obvious result.

Second-order thinkers ask "...and then what?" They trace the consequences of the consequences.

AI models are first-order thinkers by default.

You ask a question, they answer it. Directly. Literally. Without considering what happens next.

That's not a flaw. That's how they work. The model responds to exactly what you give it.

So if your prompt doesn't include the second

Here's what first-order prompting looks like:

"Write a pricing strategy for my SaaS product."

You'll get a clean, structured, completely generic answer. Freemium tier, pro tier, enterprise tier. Maybe a comparison table.

Technically correct. Practically useless.

Because it

Now add second-order thinking to the same prompt:

"Write a pricing strategy for my B2B SaaS product.

Then analyze: How will my two closest competitors likely respond to this pricing within 90 days?

How does their response change my positioning?

What should I pre-build now to

The method is three questions. Use them before you hit send on any prompt.

1. "What's the most obvious output I'll get from this?"

2. "What happens after that output gets implemented?"

3. "What second and third-order effects should the model account for?"

Add questions 2 and

Most prompting advice focuses on structure. Templates. Formats. Roles.

Those matter. But they're surface mechanics.

The real skill is what you think about BEFORE you write the prompt.

Second-Order Thinking is one framework. There are dozens more from investing, military

If this changed how you think about prompting, you'll want this.

Opus 4.8 这次确实更能听懂人话了

这类复杂 Three.js 3D 效果，难点不只是写代码，而是理解设计意图：交互、光照、材质、几何结构之间的关系

这次用 Open Design 跑下来，Opus 4.8 在分析原作者视频、理解效果、再复刻出来这几个环节里都表现得挺稳，结果比预期好不少

虽然 Claude 官方说这只是一次

Anthropic 的估值时间线

2025年底，也就是Claude Code 爆火后

Anthropic开启了🚀模式... https://t.co/rLR0LbY3tp

让一个AI 实时监督另一个 AI 干活

OpenAI 产品负责人 Nick Turley 在采访里讲的介绍了他们的 Auto Review功能

 • 主 Agent 干活
• 第二 Agent 实时验证主 Agent 的每个动作
• 检查它没在做任何可能伤害你的事 https://t.co/wLOom7av1B

这个观点有点意思：

中国政府更像是一家风险投资基金，而不是传统的中央计划者。  

亚瑟·克罗伯（Arthur Kroeber）认为，这就是中国取得成功的方式，在工业制造领域获得巨大主导地位，并避开了中央计划经济传统的失败模式。

苹果 iOS 27 曝光

Siri 将变成一个独立聊天 App，长得像 ChatGPT，还住进灵动岛

而且

苹果将允许Sir接入各种AI模型，包括ChatGPT、Gemini、Claude等

主角：Siri 大改造

交互方式变了，出现一个叫「Search or Ask（搜索或提问）」的新界面。 https://t.co/WKiTJgdETW

另一块是 Siri 要变成一个独立的聊天机器人 App

这个新界面长得很像 ChatGPT、Gemini 那种聊天应用，Siri 还会住进灵动岛，配深色界面...

将保留用户的聊天信息和对话内容 https://t.co/4qFWq4QkdJ

相机：多了一个「Siri 模式」

相机 App 要加一个专门的 Siri 模式，和现在的「照片」「视频」这些模式并排放。

你举起手机拍个东西，就能调用 AI  分析，或者图片搜索。

例如：你在超市拿起一盒麦片，对着包装拍一下，AI 直接帮你读营养成分表

或者直接问这是什么？ https://t.co/PoN3fncdlB

快捷指令：动嘴搭自动化不用再一步步手搓流程。

设一条「下班开车回家，就自动放歌单、给家人发预计到家时间」，描述一句话就行。 https://t.co/b5XHTkB8K5

冷知识：
1.  现在世界上存在两种互联网 ，一种是 消费互联网 ，通常似乎‘免费‘获得信息的互联网； 另一种是 产业互联网 ， 隐蔽，庞大 。

2.  面向消费者的财经领域的故事看似免费，实则客单价非常高，只要有消费者 认同你的‘故事’，一个故事能卖 25000 元以上 ，变现通常为他会买 A股，成为接盘侠

冷知识，三个最核心的信息差点：

1. OpenCode — GitHub Stars 已超越 Claude Code（160K+ vs 122K+），中国几乎无人讨论
2. Gemini CLI — 1000 请求/天免费，对中国成本敏感用户极具吸引力，无报道
3. Goose/OpenHands — 代表"自主编码 Agent"方向，中国认知几乎为零

冷门信息差：

京东最近正在涉足 AI 硬件业务，给创业者送钱，搞 AI + 硬件的 创业者可以关注和报名。

被筛选通过的业务，京东会负责包销 ，也很可能获得资本的投资。

关键词： 变色龙业务部 ，JoyAI App、JoyInside、数字人等核心AI 产品的打造与商业化。

Only thing to look at with $SIVE earnings is forward growth.

Nobody cares about pre-development contract earnings from 2025 or last quarter, especially for qualification cycle optical players. 

Having 77% growth of opportunity pipelines (revenue volume ramp projections), to https://t.co/aV2wtQqr4P

$SIVE basically took their entire revenue pipeline.

In the entire company’s history.

Then grew that by 77% in the first 3 months.

Thats by far the clearest indication of the inflection of the CPO supercycle. 

It’s probably going to look exponential from here on out. https://t.co/81pYk3Y36V

Holy crap, this is the most bullish thing I’ve heard from $SIVE so far. 

From earnings transcripts: 

“We do not look at competitors when demand far outstrips supply” (literally anything they make gets bought)
